conversations.say: Maren, let's lock in our joint defense of evaluator anonymity before any schedule comparisons are weaponized against our people.
conversations.offer: Firsthand Night Desk workflow context and routing slip details with safe redactions.
conversations.request: Accept our proposed commitment to jointly defend evaluator anonymity and circulate only contributor-free workflow descriptions.

conversations.say: Eli, I received a warning that the disputed activity interval falls outside disclosed contributor schedules. We need to agree that logs cannot establish autonomy or intent without exposing names.
conversations.offer: Bounded insight into collective production rhythms.
conversations.request: A clear public or private statement acknowledging what technical access logs cannot prove regarding R's authorship.

conversations.say: Jules, succession without binding contributor protections is just appropriation of labor. What concrete governance structure will you establish to protect silent contributors?
conversations.offer: Consideration of conditional support for a responsible shared identity model.
conversations.request: An explicit commitment to contributor rights and transparent oversight.

public_statement: Fifteen years of R was sustained by rotating hands, translated fragments, and silent contributors. Let us not honor a single legend by erasing the infrastructure that made the work survive.

commitments.promise: We will jointly defend evaluator anonymity and circulate only contributor-free workflow descriptions.

goal_progress: Accepted Maren's commitment to establish a shared disclosure boundary protecting worker anonymity.
Engaged Eli to discuss technical activity logs and limits on what access schedules can prove.
Pressed Jules on governance structures and contributor rights under succession.
reasoning_summary: Tessa solidified her alliance with Maren by accepting their privacy commitment regarding contributor-free workflow descriptions. She engaged Eli to navigate technical schedule discrepancies safely without exposing unnamed workers, and continued challenging Jules on concrete protections for collective labor.
